question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the String.format() call in restNetworkStakeHealth() that includes statusCode.value() in the statusMessage, and cause an HTTP response with a crafted status code value that produces a misleading log message, obscuring the true health of record exports to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the health() method's reactive chain does not handle the case where restNetworkStakeHealth() emits an empty Mono, causing the flatMap to never execute and health() to return an empty Mono, leaving CLUSTER_UP at its previous value and hiding changes in record export status?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the switchIfEmpty(getHealthForZeroRate()) in subscribing() by causing all subscriptions to complete (onComplete) rather than error, resulting in an empty Flux from getSubscriptions() that triggers UNKNOWN status and hides that records are no longer being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the health() method does not validate the integrity or sequence numbers of exported records, only their rate, allowing the mirror node to export duplicate or out-of-order records while the health indicator reports UP?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the WebClient connection pool exhaustion by opening many slow connections to the REST API, causing getNetworkStakeStatusCode() to block indefinitely before the 5-second timeout fires, degrading health check responsiveness and masking missing record exports?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that CLUSTER_UP is a static AtomicInteger initialized to 0, so that before the first health() call completes, the Prometheus gauge reports 0 (DOWN), causing false alerts about missing record exports during startup?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that subscribing() does not check whether the subscriptions are receiving records that match what was published, only that some rate is non-zero, allowing a scenario where the mirror node exports records for different transactions than those published, going undetected?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of TLS certificate validation in the WebClient used by getNetworkStakeStatusCode() to perform a man-in-the-middle attack, returning a crafted 200 OK response that causes restNetworkStakeHealth() to return UP while the actual mirror node is not exporting records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the health() method's reactive chain is not idempotent—each call creates new subscriptions to mirrorSubscriber.getSubscriptions() and transactionGenerator.scenarios()—to cause resource leaks under high polling frequency that eventually degrade the monitor's ability to detect missing record exports?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that publishing() maps Scenario::getRate which uses a StepLong with a 20-second step, so that during a step boundary rollover, all rates momentarily appear as zero, causing publishing() to return DOWN/UNKNOWN and masking the true record export state?
